blocks.text: The earth is the Lord’s and all that is in it, the world, and those who live in it;

Ps 24: The awesome majesty of the house of the Lord. A hymn celebrating the ’s enthronement as king that begins, like Ps 15, with a demand of right conduct and loyalty. The psalm proceeds in three stages: the proclamation of the cosmic victory of the (vv. 1–2), a scrutiny to make certain that only the loyal enter and welcome the Divine Warrior to his dwelling (vv. 3–6), and a call to the Temple gates to welcome God the king.
for he has founded it on the seas, and established it on the rivers.

1–2 : Presupposed is the combat myth in which the storm god defeats chaotic Sea who threatens to destroy the world, creates the orderly universe, and erects a palace (temple) to commemorate the victory. Israel likely borrowed and transformed this myth from its Canaanite predecessors.
Who shall ascend the hill of the Lord? And who shall stand in his holy place?
Those who have clean hands and pure hearts, who do not lift up their souls to what is false, and do not swear deceitfully.
4 : , similar to the commandment of the Decalogue, “You shall have no other gods before you” (Ex 20.3; Deut 5.7).
They will receive blessing from the Lord, and vindication from the God of their salvation.
5 : , the deity shares the fruits of victory.
Such is the company of those who seek him, who seek the face of the God of Jacob. Selah
3–6 : Only those loyal to the victorious are fit to share the fruits of victory; the scrutiny determines whether worshipers have been loyal; cf. Ps 15.2–5.
Lift up your heads, O gates! and be lifted up, O ancient doors! that the King of glory may come in.
7 : Temple are personified and invited to lift up their heads in joy and join the chorus welcoming the triumphant Warrior; some scholars emend “gates” to “gatekeepers.”
Who is the King of glory? The Lord, strong and mighty, the Lord, mighty in battle.
Lift up your heads, O gates! and be lifted up, O ancient doors! that the King of glory may come in.
Who is this King of glory? The Lord of hosts, he is the King of glory. Selah
